economic_notes: First Nation is building a Cultural Centre and L.I.F.E. Services Building expansion. Operating loan of $20,050,000 from TD Bank financing these capital projects. Council resolution to transfer $40,000,000 in investments to Bijiinago, Noongom, Waabang Trust held separately. Williams Treaty and Islands in the Trent settlement funds held. Old Railroad Stop (gas bar and store) is an operating enterprise segment.
notes: Consolidated entity includes Zhooniya Makak GP Inc. and Zhooniya Makak LP. Large investment portfolio (~$95M) primarily consists of Williams Treaty and Islands in the Trent settlement funds held in trust/restricted. Operating loan of $20,050,000 secured by investment accounts; First Nation breached DSC covenant (5% vs required 120%) but TD Bank waived non-compliance. No separate remuneration schedule included in extracted text. Restricted cash of ~$3.1M and externally/internally restricted investments of ~$92.8M included in totals. Member distributions of $9,691,562 included in administration expenses representing treaty settlement distributions. Accounts receivable calculated as Due from Government ($1,066,504) plus Other accounts receivable ($191,519). Current liabilities calculated as Accounts payable ($1,869,652) plus Due to Government ($1,915,918). Long-term debt is classified as operating loan on balance sheet. OSR calculated as total revenue minus total government transfers ($27,270,979 - $8,898,369 = $18,372,610) but includes investment income and Old Railroad Stop revenue.
